Off Calendar Mar-10-2026 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Mar-03-2026 Continued To Mar-10-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Mar-25-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Maureen McFadden for Alfred Wall, Petitioner (via Zoom).

The Court inquired into Probate Code Section 6124 and whether the presumption of revocation applied. Ms. McFadden confirmed that the decedent was of sound mind at the time of death and had not expressed any intent to revoke the will. She described her prior representation of the decedent and his wife in 2016 when they executed the subject will, and noted the decedent's longstanding plan to divide the estate equally between his and his wife's heirs. Based on Ms. McFadden's statements, the Court found the presumption under Section 6124 had been overcome.

The court granted the Petition for Probate of Lost Will. The lost will dated June 8, 2016, is admitted to probate. Alfred Wall is appointed executor with full IAEA authority. No bond is required. A status hearing for filing of a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is set for April 7, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. If either petition is filed two weeks prior, no appearance is required on that date. Michael Kasolas is appointed probate referee. Order for the same signed in open Court.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Mar-19-2025 Continued To Mar-25-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Maureen McFadden, Esq., for Alfred Wall, Petitioner.

At the court's query, Ms. McFadden reports as to item #8 of the instant petition. The court advises that it intends to continue this matter to June-03-2023 to allow counsel to file a supplement. Counsel offers advocacy for a shorter continuance advising that she can file the supplement by the end of this week. The court considers the preceding and the matter is continued to Mar-25-2025 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: . Not Reported.
